source.title: Association of IRF5 rs2004640 polymorphism and systemic lupus erythematosus: A meta-analysis

description: IRF5 rs2004640 is a functional G/T polymorphism that alters splicing and increases interferon-alpha production. The T allele is associated with increased risk of Sjogren syndrome and systemic lupus erythematosus.

GG.phenotype: Lower SLE and Sjogren's disease risk
GG.recommendation: Standard health maintenance; family counseling if autoimmune disease in family

GT.recommendation: Baseline ANA and anti-Ro/La antibody screening; monitor for symptoms (dry eyes, dry mouth, joint pain); periodic rheumatologic evaluation if symptoms

TG.recommendation: Baseline ANA and anti-Ro/La antibody screening; monitor for symptoms (dry eyes, dry mouth, joint pain); periodic rheumatologic evaluation if symptoms

TT.phenotype: Elevated SLE and Sjogren's disease risk
TT.effect: Risk allele homozygote; substantially increased autoimmune risk across populations
TT.recommendation: Comprehensive autoimmune screening (ANA, anti-Ro/La, anti-Smith, complement levels); regular rheumatology monitoring; ophthalmology evaluation for ocular involvement; renal function monitoring
